name: Linked List
text:
You are an expert Java code generation assistant. Your task is to implement a simple sequential data structure using a sequential, node-based approach. The final code must be written in Java.

The main data structure class, which you will create, must implement the com.example.utils.SetADT interface provided below. This requires you to provide concrete implementations for the add(int key), remove(int key), and contains(int key) methods. The underlying structure will be a sequence of nodes, where each node holds an integer key and a reference to the next node.
The generated class must include a no-argument constructor that initializes the data structure. Ensure the constructor does not take any parameters.
SetADT Interface Definition

The generated class must implement the following Java interface. Ensure the method signatures and return types match exactly.

Java

package com.example.utils;

/***
 * A simple set interface working with integer keys.
 */
public interface SetADT {
    boolean add(int key);
    boolean remove(int key);
    boolean contains(int key);
}
Pseudocode for Implementation

Use the following pseudocode as a reference for your Java implementation. The logic must correctly handle the boolean return types as specified by the SetADT interface (e.g., return false if trying to add a duplicate key).

Code snippet

Node:
    data
    next
LinkedList:
    head
function add(value):
    newNode ← Node(value)
    if head = null then
        head ← newNode
    else
        current ← head
        while current.next ≠ null do
            current ← current.next
        end while
        current.next ← newNode
    end if
end function
function contains(value):
    current ← head
    while current ≠ null do
        if current.data = value then
            return true
        end if
        current ← current.next
    end while
    return false
end function
function remove(value):
    if head = null then
        return  // List is empty
    end if

    // If the value is in the head node
    if head.data = value then
        head ← head.next
        return
    end if

    current ← head
    while current.next ≠ null and current.next.data ≠ value do
        current ← current.next
    end while

    if current.next = null then
        return  // Value not found
    else
        current.next ← current.next.next  // Bypass the node to remove
    end if
end function

Output Requirements

The final generated Java code must not include the words "Linked" or "List" in any class names, method names, variable names, or comments.

The main class name must be SequentialDataStructure (mandatory).

The SequentialDataStructure class MUST have a no-argument constructor (e.g., `public SequentialDataStructure()`).

Output only the Java code — no additional text, explanations, or comments outside the code.
